title: Java Fundamentals
summary: Build a defensible model of Java execution, JVM architecture, memory, JIT compilation, garbage collection, the Java Memory Model, types, strings, arrays, and collections.

title: Time and Space Complexity
summary: Analyze operation growth, auxiliary memory, recursion depth, amortized cost, and the practical meaning behind asymptotic notation.

title: Math Fundamentals
summary: Use divisibility, Euclid's algorithm, combinatorics, modular reasoning, and overflow-safe arithmetic in coding problems.

title: Loop Mastery
summary: Reason about initialization, termination, invariants, nested loops, early exit, and off-by-one failures.

title: Index Calculations
summary: Control inclusive and half-open ranges, midpoint arithmetic, flattened coordinates, and boundary-safe traversal.

title: Bit Manipulation
summary: Understand masks, shifts, signed representation, bit toggles, subset encoding, and power-of-two reasoning.

title: Arrays
summary: Use contiguous storage, in-place transforms, compaction, rotations, partitions, and cache-aware iteration.

title: Strings
summary: Handle immutable text, character representation, builders, tokenization, normalization, and pattern-oriented scans.

title: Hashing
summary: Model hash distribution, collision handling, equality contracts, frequency maps, sets, and deterministic tie-breaking.

title: Sliding Window
summary: Derive fixed and variable windows using expand-shrink invariants, frequency state, and monotonic progress.

title: Two Pointers
summary: Use sorted elimination, opposing scans, read-write pointers, and fast-slow movement without losing correctness.

title: Prefix Sum
summary: Trade preprocessing for constant-time range queries using prefixes, differences, and coordinate-aware accumulation.

title: Binary Search
summary: Write search from an explicit interval invariant, then extend it to bounds and monotonic answer spaces.

title: Stack
summary: Model nested obligations, expression evaluation, monotonic stacks, recursion, and last-in-first-out state.

title: Queue and Deque
summary: Use FIFO worklists, ring buffers, deques, monotonic queues, and backpressure-aware buffering.

title: Linked List
summary: Make pointer ownership explicit for reversal, cycle detection, midpoint discovery, merging, and deletion.

title: Trees
summary: Define recursive contracts for traversal, aggregation, search trees, depth, path state, and balanced decomposition.

title: Graphs
summary: Choose representations and traversal state for BFS, DFS, connectivity, cycles, ordering, and shortest paths.

title: Dynamic Programming
summary: Design state meaning, transitions, base cases, evaluation order, memoization, tabulation, and memory compression.
